item.title: 1944-11-28 – Greeley Daily Tribune – Leslie Dauths Host Jeremiassen-Dauth Thanksgiving
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1944-11-28 – Greeley Daily Tribune – Leslie Dauths Host Jeremiassen-Dauth Thanksgiving appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1944-11-28 – Greeley Daily Tribune – Leslie Dauths Host Jeremiassen-Dauth Thanksgiving

Transcription
"Mr. and Mrs. Jens Jeremiassen were dinner guests of Dr. and Mrs. Andre Anderson. Mr and Mrs. J. Donald Sivers, Ross and Sue Sivers, Ross Jeremiassen, Mr. and Mrs. John Jeremiassen, Jr., and daughters of Ault, Miss Else Dauth of Denver, Mr. and Mrs. George Dauth of Greeley were Thanksgiving dinner guests of Mrs. Les Dauth, Mr. and Mrs. John Jeremiassen of Greeley."

People
Elsie Dauth
George Dauth
Florence Yeaton
Leslie Dauth
Jens Jeremiassen
Ane Andersen
Mildred Jeremiassen
Donald Sivers
Ross Sivers
Sue Sivers
Ross Jeremiassen
Marie RIshoi
John Jeremiassen
Karen Rasmussen

Details
Date: 1944-11-28
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: Greeley Daily Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1944-11-28
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/article/1944-11-28-greeley-daily-tribune-leslie-dauths-host-jeremiassen-dauth-thanksgiving/

The post 1944-11-28 – Greeley Daily Tribune – Leslie Dauths Host Jeremiassen-Dauth Thanksgiving appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1943-12-31 – Greeley Daily Tribune – Elsie Dauth Spends Christmas With George Dauth Family
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1943-12-31 – Greeley Daily Tribune – Elsie Dauth Spends Christmas With George Dauth Family appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1943-12-31 – Greeley Daily Tribune – Elsie Dauth Spends Christmas With George Dauth Family

Transcription
"Miss Elsie Dauth of Denver, will spend the New Year holiday with her aunt and uncle Mr. and Mrs. George Dauth, 1731 Eleventh avenue."

People
Elsie Dauth
George Dauth
Florence Yeaton

Details
Date: 1943-12-31
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley

item.content:encoded:
1932-11-23 – Greeley Daily Tribune – Elsie Dauth Visits George Dauth Family

Transcription
"Mr. and Mrs. George Dauth are to have as guests Mr. and Mrs. Lee Alden of Brighton. Mr. and Mrs. Philip Dauth of Denver, Bernice Alden and Elsie Dauth."

George Dauth
Florence Yeaton
Louise Dauth

item.title: 1931-11-19 – Greeley Daily Tribune – Elsie Dauth at Louise Alden Party
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1931-11-19 – Greeley Daily Tribune – Elsie Dauth at Louise Alden Party appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1931-11-19 – Greeley Daily Tribune – Elsie Dauth at Louise Alden Party

Transcription
"Mr. and Mrs. Lee Alden had as their guests Sunday Mr. and Mrs. George Dauth and son Leslie from Greeley. Elsie Dauth from Denver and Mrs. Marie Horning from California. Mrs. Horning and Mrs. Dauth are aunts of Mrs. Alden and Elsie Dauth is a sister. "

People
Elsie Dauth
George Dauth
Florence Yeaton
Louise Dauth
Lee Alden

Details
Date: 1931-11-19
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1931-11-19

item.title: 1937-05-26 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauths Trees At Glenmere Park
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1937-05-26 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauths Trees At Glenmere Park appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1937-05-26 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauths Trees At Glenmere Park

Transcription
New Oil Mat Area Covers Six BLocks
"On motion of George Davis council voted that committee power to purchase form George Dauth 100 evergreen trees for $150 for Glenmere Park, Dauth to guarantee that 90 percent live and replace those that died from last year's purchase from Dauth."

People
George Dauth

Details
Date: 1937-05-26
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: "New Oil Mat Area Covers Six BLocks." The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1937-05-26
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/article/1937-05-26-greeley-daily-tribune-george-dauths-trees-at-glenmere-park/

The post 1937-05-26 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauths Trees At Glenmere Park appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1937-03-18 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauths North Game Refuge
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1937-03-18 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauths North Game Refuge appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1937-03-18 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauths North Game Refuge

Transcription
White Swan Flies Away from North Game Refuge
"One of the four white swans kept at the George Dauth game refuge north of the Greeley city limits near highway 85 has flown away, it was learned today. The swan left during the storm last week end. George Dauth said here that he expects the swan to return. The missing bird is one of the young ones reared last season by the original pair belonging to Bruce Eaton. The two old birds and one young one are still at the refuge."

People
George Dauth

Details
Date: 1937-03-18
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: "White Swan Flies Away from North Game Refuge." The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1937-03-18
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/article/1937-03-18-greeley-daily-tribune-george-dauths-north-game-refuge/

The post 1937-03-18 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauths North Game Refuge appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1936-04-29 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauth Provides Trees For Greeley
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1936-04-29 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauth Provides Trees For Greeley appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1936-04-29 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauth Provides Trees For Greeley

Transcription
(Continued from Page 1)
"...city had expended $871.14 for water line extensions and system installations in the park as a WPA project. System installed, including labor, is considered worth $4,055.55, according to a tabulation presented. Willows will be planted on the island in the lake. Park committee was instructed to purchase about 50 evergreens for planting in the park. They will cost $1.50 each and will be three to four fee thigh. George Dauth will furnish them."

People
George Dauth

Details
Date: 1936-04-29
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: "(Continued from Page 1)." The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1936-04-29
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/article/1936-04-29-greeley-daily-tribune-george-dauth-provides-trees-for-greeley/

The post 1936-04-29 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauth Provides Trees For Greeley appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1934-04-02 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauth Trustee Of BPOE
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1934-04-02 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauth Trustee Of BPOE appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1934-04-02 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauth Trustee Of BPOE

Transcription
"Greeley lodge No. 17, B.P.O.E. installed officers for the ensuing year at their meeting last night. The following were installed: Charles B. Littell, exalted ruler; O. J. Fisher, leading knight; Judge John C. Nixon, loyal knight; Claude Newton, lecturing knight; H. C. Cooper secretary; C. C. Neill, treasurer, and George Dauth, Reelected as trustee."

People
George Dauth

Details
Date: 1934-04-02
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1934-04-02
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/article/1934-04-02-greeley-daily-tribune-george-dauth-trustee-of-bpoe/

The post 1934-04-02 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauth Trustee Of BPOE appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1931-09-21 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauth Ranch
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1931-09-21 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauth Ranch appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1931-09-21 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauth Ranch
Advertisement that provides the location of George Dauth's Ranch.

Transcription
Thursday, Sept. 24th on the Old Dauth Ranch
"1/2 MI. SOUTH, 1 1/2 WEST OF KERSEY; 5 1/2 MILES DUE EAST OF EVANS; 6 HORSES: Brown mare, wt., 1400; brown gelding, smooth, wt., 1500; black gelding, smooth, 1500; grey mare, smooth, wt., 1460; saddle pony; sucking colt. 32 CATTLE: 15 head high grade Holstein cows, 1 red cow, 1 spotted cow. These are extra good dairy cows and in fine condition. 1 yearling Holstein bull, 6 coming 2-year-old heifers, 9 calves. 2 tacks alfalfa hay.; IMPLEMENTS: Wagon, hay rack, beet bed, grain binder, 2 way plow, harrow, field ditcher, mower, 3 sets harness, bean cultivator, potato cultivator, P & O irater, corn and bean planter, spring tooth, 1925 Ford truck with beat bed and hay rack, small tools of various kinds and other articles.; Be sure to attend this sale for good dairy cows and anything you may need in farm equipment."

People
George Dauth

Details
Date: 1931-09-21
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: "Thursday, Sept. 24th on the Old Dauth Ranch." The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1931-09-21
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/article/1931-09-21-greeley-daily-tribune-george-dauth-ranch/

The post 1931-09-21 – Greeley Daily Tribune – George Dauth Ranch appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
